---
title: "notebook"
output: html_document
date: "2023-09-25"
---

**Ideas:**
- algatr pipeline - GDM (+ kmeans clustering), MMRR, LFMM, RDA
- wingen
- DUI/IUD/UDI?
- EEMS
- Human footprint vs ROH - idea: geographic weighted regression to see where human impacts have a larger affect than expected (for example, does pi affect response?)
- Suitability vs ROH

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)

merger <- data.frame(Company1 = c("A", "B"),
                     Company2 = c("C", "D"),
                     # note that in this example Company 1 always takes over Company 2
                     # however, this is not necessary
                     FinalCompany = c("A", "B"),
                     MergerDate = c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01"))

# we only need the FinalCompany and the MergerDate for our calculations
merger <- merger %>% select(MergerDate, FinalCompany)

merger <- merger %>% mutate(MergerDate = as.Date(MergerDate))

print(merger)

companies <- data.frame(Company = c("A", "B", "C", "D", 
                                    "A", "B", "C", "D"),
                        Score = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 
                                  2, 4, 6, 8,
                                  4, 8, NA, 16,
                                  8, 16, NA, NA),
                        ScoreDate = c("2019-01-01", "2019-01-01", "2019-01-01", "2019-01-01",
                                      "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01",
                                      "2021-01-01", "2021-01-01", NA, "2021-01-01",
                                      "2022-01-01", "2022-01-01", NA, NA))

companies <- companies %>% mutate(ScoreDate = as.Date(ScoreDate))

print(companies)

change <-
  # join companies by the final company name
  left_join(companies, merger, by = c("Company" = "FinalCompany")) %>%
  # make new columns for the year before and the year after the merger
  mutate(OneYearBefore = MergerDate - years(1),
         OneYearAfter = MergerDate + years(1))

# Filter the data to keep only the rows corresponding to the closest scores
filtered_data <- 
  change %>%
  filter(ScoreDate >= OneYearBefore & ScoreDate <= OneYearAfter)

# Calculate the change in scores
result <- 
  filtered_data %>%
  group_by(Company) %>%
  summarize(
    ChangeInScore = last(Score) - first(Score)
  )

  
  
```
